w:r.w:t: 🏭 S.A.G.A. BLUEPRINT 01: The Lore Database (The Vault)

A standalone Python microservice running on its own local port (e.g., 8001). Its sole responsibility is to ingest an Obsidian markdown vault, categorize the files, generate mathematical embeddings (vectors), and serve search results via a REST API.
sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (Runs locally, completely free, no API keys needed).
python-frontmatter (Extracts YAML tags from Obsidian files).
To keep this module 100% independent, it gets its own folder and environment.
│ └── .chroma/ # Where the vector database physically lives
├── vault_parser.py # Logic for reading Obsidian .md files
├── vector_store.py # The ChromaDB client and embedding logic
└── auto_categorizer.py # Fallback logic for un-tagged files
This is the unbreakable law of how data enters and leaves the module. We use Pydantic to ensure the API rejects bad data.
# The exact categories allowed in the SAGA engine
"Absolute local path to the Obsidian Vault"
"If true, wipes the existing DB before ingest"
# The mathematical relevance score (lower is better)
Use os.walk or pathlib to recursively find every .md file in the provided vault_path.
Use the frontmatter.load(filepath) library. This magically splits the file into post.metadata (the YAML header) and post.content (the actual lore).
If it is missing or invalid
, pass the post.content to the auto_categorizer.py.
Since we want this fast and local, we use keyword heuristics first. If that fails, it defaults to LORE.
# ... expand this heuristic list for all categories ...
This is the heart of the module. It must initialize ChromaDB with persistent storage so it doesn't forget the lore when you turn it off.
# The 'where' clause is how ChromaDB filters strictly by your Obsidian tags
We use FastAPI to expose the internal logic to the rest of the factory line.
# Format raw_results into the SearchResponse schema and return
6. How to Build & Run This Exact Module
If you create saga_lore_module/, write requirements.txt containing:
You now have an independent API running on port 8001.
Your Game Master App can send an HTTP POST to localhost:8001/api/lore/search asking for PLANT lore, and it will return perfectly formatted JSON instantly, utilizing semantic vector math.
If you completely delete your Game Master App, this Lore Database will still run perfectly on its own. Total modularity.
